title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After a doctor determines that Moira has been administered an opiate, he suggests she be sent to a nearby private sanatorium. Bobby and Frankie agree and move to the Seven Stars Inn, while Inspector Hammond remains skeptical of their claims regarding Roger Baskington-French's escape. During the night, Frankie informs Bobby that she has found evidence of being chloroformed, which she has hidden in a coal bucket.

The following morning, Bobby informs Frankie that Inspector Hammond is treating the recent events as a prank. Despite this, Bobby remains determined to investigate the death of John Savage, suspecting that either his will is a forgery or that he was murdered, with Roger Baskington-French potentially involved. Frankie notes that the witnesses to the Savage will were the chef, Ross Chadley, and the gardener, Albert Merri, while the lawyer is from the firm Elford & Sprague.

Bobby shows Frankie a photograph of a Mr. Kemman found behind a phone. A waitress identifies the man in the photo as Mr. Templeton, the owner of Tudor Cottage, who recently moved abroad after inheriting a large fortune. Bobby and Frankie decide to split up to investigate. Frankie visits the lawyer, Mr. Elford, using a ruse to ask about the Savage will. Elford insists the will is legitimate, as he drew it up while Savage was present and witnessed by the servants and the gardener, making forgery unlikely. During their lunch meeting, Bobby reveals to Frankie that the gardener, Albert Merri, has died, though they speculate his death might have been natural due to his age.
